message:
feat(highlight): add terminal formatter for caret-style span highlighting

Add a new highlight module that provides caret-style visual highlighting
for showing which parts of a command matched destructive patterns in
terminal output.

Key features:
- HighlightSpan struct for defining highlight regions (byte offsets)
- HighlightedCommand struct with command line, caret line, and label
- UTF-8 safe span rendering via character-to-byte offset conversion
- ANSI color support with automatic TTY detection
- NO_COLOR and TERM=dumb environment variable support
- Command windowing integration for long commands via evaluator module
- Multi-span highlighting support for commands with multiple matches

The module integrates with the evaluator's window_command() function
for UTF-8 safe windowing of long commands while preserving accurate
caret positioning under the matched span.

Example output format:
  Command: <dangerous command here>
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
           └── Matched: <pattern name>

Includes 16 unit tests covering span creation, formatting, windowing,
UTF-8 handling, edge cases, and color output modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","title":"[PACK] platform.gitlab - GitLab Platform Pack","description":"# GitLab Platform Pack (Non-CI Operations)

## Scope Clarification
This pack covers GitLab **platform-wide** operations that are NOT covered by `cicd.gitlab_ci`:
- Project/repository management
- Releases
- Protected branches
- Webhooks
- Group management

**The `cicd.gitlab_ci` pack covers:**
- CI/CD variables
- Pipelines
- Runners

## Destructive Patterns (Platform-specific)

### Project Operations
- `glab repo delete` - Project deletion
- `glab repo archive` - Archive project

### Release Operations
- `glab release delete` - Deletes releases

### API Operations
- `glab api -X DELETE projects/` - Project deletion
- `glab api -X DELETE .../protected_branches/` - Remove protection
- `glab api -X DELETE .../hooks/` - Webhook deletion

### Admin Operations (self-hosted)
- `gitlab-rails runner` with destructive code
- `gitlab-rake` destructive maintenance tasks

## Safe Patterns
- `glab repo view`, `glab repo list`, `glab repo clone`
- `glab mr list`, `glab mr view`
- `glab issue list`, `glab issue view`
- `glab api` with GET method

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: platform.gitlab (git_safety_guard-h5j)
- Keywords: glab, gitlab-rails, gitlab-rake
- IMPORTANT: Do NOT duplicate patterns from cicd.gitlab_ci

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests in `src/packs/platform/gitlab.rs`
- [ ] E2E tests in `scripts/e2e_tests/platform_gitlab.txt`
- [ ] Verify no overlap with cicd.gitlab_ci patterns
- [ ] Test project/release operations
- [ ] Performance < 500µs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Project deletion blocked
- [ ] Release deletion blocked
- [ ] Protected branch removal blocked
- [ ] No duplicate patterns with cicd.gitlab_ci
- [ ] Completion checklist passed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:33:47.879121793-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:00:42.277384353-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:00:42.277384353-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented platform.gitlab pack + unit tests + added E2E coverage in scripts/e2e_test.sh","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.943656825-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:38.988017189-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.031117693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.074253284-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.116035858-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.157638945-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.553226423-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.598241766-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Use cwd-based project override in enabled_pack_ids and add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T16:27:57.235782465-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:28:28.357186979-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:28:28.357186979-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-z72c","title":"Allow-once codes: Switch from hex to numeric-only format","notes":"
---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_numeric_code_generation() {
    // Generate 1000 codes and verify all are 5-digit numeric
    for _ in 0..1000 {
        let code = generate_bypass_code();
        assert_eq!(code.len(), 5);
        assert!(code.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()));
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_code_uniqueness() {
    // Generate 10000 codes and check collision rate
    let mut codes = std::collections::HashSet::new();
    for _ in 0..10000 {
        codes.insert(generate_bypass_code());
    }
    // With 100000 possible codes, expect <1% collision rate
    assert!(codes.len() > 9900);
}

#[test]
fn test_code_validation() {
    assert!(is_valid_bypass_code("12345"));
    assert!(is_valid_bypass_code("00000"));
    assert!(is_valid_bypass_code("99999"));
    assert!(!is_valid_bypass_code("1234"));   // Too short
    assert!(!is_valid_bypass_code("123456")); // Too long
    assert!(!is_valid_bypass_code("1234a"));  // Contains letter
    assert!(!is_valid_bypass_code("abcde"));  // All letters
}

#[test]
fn test_code_storage_and_lookup() {
    let store = PendingExceptions::new_temp();
    let code = store.add_exception("test command", Duration::from_secs(3600));

    assert!(store.validate_and_consume(&code));
    assert!(!store.validate_and_consume(&code)); // Can only use once
}
```

### Integration Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_bypass_flow_with_numeric_code() {
    // 1. Block a command
    let result = evaluate_command("dangerous command");
    assert_eq!(result.outcome, Outcome::Deny);

    // 2. Get the bypass code from output
    let code = result.bypass_code.unwrap();
    assert!(code.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()));

    // 3. Use code to bypass
    let bypass_result = bypass_with_code(&code);
    assert!(bypass_result.is_ok());
}
```

### E2E Tests

```bash
# Test numeric code is displayed on block
OUTPUT=$(echo '{"command": "dangerous"}' | dcg hook --stdin 2>&1)
CODE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oE '[0-9]{5}')
[[ ${#CODE} -eq 5 ]] || exit 1
echo "PASS: 5-digit numeric code displayed"

# Test code can be used for bypass
dcg bypass "$CODE" && echo "PASS: Bypass accepted" || exit 1
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T00:39:35.221357607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T07:16:04.767977831-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-14T07:16:04.767977831-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 5-digit numeric short codes: modified short_code_from_hash to convert last 8 hex chars to decimal mod 100000, added comprehensive tests for format validation, uniqueness, edge cases, and leading zeros preservation. All 32 pending_exceptions tests pass."}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-zpw8","title":"[Explain] Integrate explanations into hook and CLI output","description":"# Task: Integrate explanations into hook and CLI output

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Display pattern explanations in both hook denial output and dcg explain/test commands, providing users with context about why their command was blocked.

## Integration Points

### 1. Hook Denial Output (src/hook.rs)

Add explanation section to the denial box:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⛔ BLOCKED by DCG                                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Pattern: core.git:reset-hard                                │
│                                                             │
│ Why blocked:                                                │
│   git reset --hard discards all uncommitted changes.        │
│   Changes that were never committed are permanently lost.   │
│                                                             │
│ Suggestions:                                                │
│   • Use git stash to save changes first                     │
│   • Use git reset --soft to preserve changes                │
│   • Preview: git diff && git status                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

### 2. dcg explain Command (src/cli.rs)

The explain command already shows detailed information. Enhance with:
- Full explanation text
- Structured suggestions list
- Documentation links

### 3. dcg test Command

Brief version of explanation for quick testing.

## Implementation Steps

1. Modify hook denial formatter to include explanation
2. Update dcg explain to show full explanation
3. Update dcg test to show brief explanation
4. Handle missing explanations gracefully (show nothing extra)
5. Ensure formatting fits terminal width

## Text Wrapping

Explanations may be multiple lines. Use textwrap crate or similar to wrap at terminal width (default 80 columns).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Hook denials show explanation when available
- dcg explain shows full explanation with suggestions
- Missing explanations handled gracefully
- Text wraps correctly at terminal width

## Dependencies

- Task: Add explanation field to DestructivePattern
- At least some pattern explanations written
